$comment: Declares the single shared tier and how each profile consumes it. The mirror-parity gate (scripts/Test-ProfileMirrorParity.ps1) reads this file. A 'sharedAsset' is one shared-tier file that BOTH profiles consume by relative path -- editing it must update both profiles, never one. A 'toolSpecificExempt' asset is a deliberately tool-specific part (a Claude-only or Codex-only skill, a launcher mechanic, a hot-rules filename) that is NEVER required to mirror. If both profile-local skill roots contain the same skill name, the gate treats it as shared by default unless listed in profileLocalSameNameExempt with a tool-specific reason. The 39-vs-34 Claude/Codex skill-count delta is the SUM of these exempt entries, not drift.

sharedAssets.name: design-contract
sharedAssets.why: One vocabulary, one set of safety rules, one report format. Section numbers are cited verbatim by both profiles' READMEs.

sharedAssets.name: forbidden-paths
sharedAssets.why: The single machine-readable forbidden surface consumed by both profiles' containment guards. One edit re-protects both profiles.

sharedAssets.name: skill-finish-to-done
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic finish-to-done skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-root-goal-check
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic root-goal-check skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-breakthrough-opportunity-review
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic hidden-opportunity review skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles can find root-goal-critical opportunities, label evidence, and write resumable opportunity reports without drifting.

sharedAssets.name: skill-easy-briefing
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic easy-briefing skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-parallel-ticket-planner
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic parallel-ticket-planner skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-mission-control
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic mission-control entrypoint. Authored once in the shared tier so non-developer students and maintainers can give one large goal while both profiles route child sessions, goal companions, data quality, validation, learning, PR/merge, and gradient optimization the same way.

sharedAssets.name: skill-ticket-issue
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic ticket-issue skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-learning-loop
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic learning-loop skill. Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both.

sharedAssets.name: skill-goal-pair-guardian
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic 목표동행 long-goal skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles check drift, early stopping, continuation state, and improvement routing with the same contract.

sharedAssets.name: skill-long-research-gradient
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic long research gradient skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles turn deep research into evidence sprints and close each sprint by reducing tokens, manager intervention, time, money, quality gaps, and recurrence risk.

sharedAssets.name: skill-ui-ux-design-guidance
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic UI/UX design guidance skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles read target repo docs/design/DESIGN.md first, fall back to the installed Driftless default guide when needed, and never encode machine-specific design paths.

sharedAssets.name: skill-insight-inbox-starter
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic public-safe insight-inbox starter skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles can adapt the pattern as a manual queue, review contract, and ledger without claiming Driftless ships the private app or live integrations.

sharedAssets.name: skill-apply-driftless
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic auto-apply skill ('apply this repo to my Claude/Codex'). One procedure both profiles follow to install/verify/report the isolated profile, ask-before-install, never touching host-global config.

sharedAssets.name: skill-adopt-external-tool
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic 'vet an external repo/tool/skill/MCP before applying it' safety checklist (scan-before-you-trust). Authored once in the shared tier; both profiles consume it by relative path, so one edit updates both. No new dependency or scanner -- a one-screen checklist closed with an ADOPT_SMALL / PILOT_ONLY / WATCH_LATER / REJECT / UNVERIFIED verdict.

sharedAssets.name: skill-safety-guard
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic beginner safety guard. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles protect secrets, host-global profiles, credentials, billing, public release, destructive action, and user data the same way.

sharedAssets.name: skill-review-before-done
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic final review skill. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles review bugs, regressions, safety, and missing evidence before PR_READY, merge, close, or release.

sharedAssets.name: skill-work-ledger
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic goal/criteria/evidence ledger. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles keep manager-readable done criteria and verification evidence.

sharedAssets.name: skill-handoff-guard
sharedAssets.why: Tool-agnostic long-session handoff guard. Authored once in the shared tier so both profiles can resume overnight work without raw logs or stale claims.

profileConsumerProof.$comment: Each profile must prove it actually consumes the shared tier (not silently re-copy it). The gate checks that each profile's README references the shared tier by relative path. A profile that stops pointing at the shared tier has forked = drift.

toolSpecificExempt.name: skill-ultracode-orchestration
toolSpecificExempt.why: Claude-only multi-agent / Workflow orchestration skill. Exists only in the Claude profile by design and is NEVER required to mirror into Codex.

toolSpecificExempt.name: skill-goal-mode
toolSpecificExempt.why: Codex-only goal-mode run prompt / driving paradigm. Exists only in the Codex profile by design and is NEVER required to mirror into Claude.

toolSpecificExempt.name: claude-only-skills
toolSpecificExempt.why: Claude-specific skills (Workflow / dynamic-workflow / Chrome DevTools MCP dependent). These exist only in the Claude profile by design and are NEVER required to mirror into Codex.

toolSpecificExempt.name: codex-only-skills
toolSpecificExempt.why: Codex-specific skills (goal-mode / openai.yaml registration dependent). These exist only in the Codex profile by design and are NEVER required to mirror into Claude.

toolSpecificExempt.name: hot-rules-filename
toolSpecificExempt.why: CLAUDE.md (Claude) vs AGENTS.md (Codex). Different filenames, same role. Intentionally tool-specific per the contract section 7.

toolSpecificExempt.name: launcher-mechanics
toolSpecificExempt.why: CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (Claude) vs CODEX_HOME (Codex). Different isolation env var, same isolation principle. Intentionally tool-specific per the contract section 7.

skillCountDelta.$comment: The Claude profile ships 39 skills; the Codex profile ships 34. This 5-delta is the difference between the two toolSpecificExempt skill buckets above. It is INTENTIONAL tool-specific specialization, not drift. The gate enforces parity on the SHARED tier only; it never forces the two skill counts to match.
